data.content:
I can see the project — an MSTest project targeting .NET 8. The express test expects `15.00` but the method returns `10.00`, so that one will fail.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET 8 MSTest project. I can start `dotnet watch test` which will re-run tests whenever files change.

Let me start it up in async mode so the user can see the output and iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-1ea839f0ae4e4d7e8cd6a498534d4e1f && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher is live in shell **`watch`**. Every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, tests will re-run automatically. To fix the failing test, change `"express" => 10.00m` to `"express" => 15.00m` on line 24. Want me to fix it?
